title: Balancing Equations
learningOutcomes: Perform the same operation on both sides.

objectives.text: Balance the scale by removing equal tiles from both sides.

theory: An equation is like a balance scale. Whatever you do to one side, you must do to the other. If you add 3 to the left side, you must add 3 to the right side too.
formulae: \text{If } A = B \text{ then } A + k = B + k
\text{If } A = B \text{ then } A - k = B - k
hints: Try adding the same tile to BOTH sides of the scale.

tasks.instruction: Make both sides equal by adding or removing tiles.

keyConcepts: The Balance Principle: same operation on both sides.
Equality is preserved by balanced operations.
commonMistakes: Changing only one side of the equation.
